title: abutilon viscosum
intro: Abutilon viscosum, also known as the sticky abutilon or sticky weed, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) that is native to tropical Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving cough and respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. In herbal preparations, Abutilon viscosum can be used to make a decoction, which is often consumed as a tea, or it can be used in the form of a paste or powder to treat various ailments.
